place: East Norwich CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: East Norwich got its name when the postal service changed it from Norwich to avoid confusion with another town in upstate New York.
The town sits atop the Harbor Hill Moraine, a glacial ridge that rises to Harbor Hill, Nassau County’s highest point.
In the late 1830s the Long Island Railroad reached nearby Hicksville, marking the first rail link that would later serve the East Norwich area.
East Norwich experiences a mix of humid subtropical and humid continental weather, with hot, humid summers and cool, snowy winters.
All children in East Norwich attend the Oyster Bay–East Norwich public schools, a district that serves the entire North Shore community.
New York State Routes 25A and 106 cut through East Norwich, connecting it to the broader Long Island corridor.
